_doc:
Calibrated session capacity for POINTS-BUDGETED batches (docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md -> 'Running a batch'). Estimator = the POOLED AFFINE context-cost model (backlog #1505): a Deming/errors-in-variables fit of context% = overhead + cost*points over EVERY sample's raw (points, contextPct) in the last 12 (capacity-bound and work-bound alike — stop-reason is audit metadata only, no longer a training gate; the old exclusion gate starved the estimate since work-bound is the common case). 'fit' caches {overhead, cost, n, residualStd}; 'capacityPoints' = implied points at 100% context = (100-overhead)/cost (display/back-compat). The batch budget 'budgetPoints' = the largest P that keeps predicted context under 'contextCeiling' minus a data-driven margin marginK*residualStd: P = round((contextCeiling - overhead - marginK*residualStd)/cost) -- replacing the old capacityPoints*targetFraction. 'points' = batchCost (a story's size; a task = 2). `node scripts/backlog.mjs calibrate --points=

--context-pct= [--stop-reason=...]` appends a sample and refits. The 12-sample window ages out old regimes (a new model); RLS-with-forgetting is the planned successor (#1516). Don't hand-edit fit/capacityPoints/budgetPoints -- let calibrate move them.
